title: penstemon fruticosus subsp. scouleri
intro: Penstemon fruticosus subsp. scouleri, also known as Scouler's penstemon, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Scouler's penstemon include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and easing respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Scouler's penstemon can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
